Notes: 1 AM departure via SCTEX-TPLEX. Minimal traffic. ~7 hrs drive. Refuel along the way.

Drop luggage and freshen up. Quick change for afternoon activities.

Notes: Bagnet Rice Bowl (₱120) or Combo meal (₱60). Budget-friendly Filipino comfort food.
Afternoon surf session! 2-hr lesson with instructor + board rental. Afternoon waves still beginner-friendly.

Location: The Great Northwest Travel Stop, Urbiztondo
Notes: Dirty Horchata (₱160) or Cold Brew Latte (₱160). Famous coffee spot.
Greek cuisine. Authentic Mediterranean food. ₱₱-₱₱₱ range.
Early rest for next day activities.

Location: MacArthur Highway, Great Northwest Travel Stop
Notes: Healthy smoothie bowls. Opens 8AM. Surfer's Bowl or Matcha Chia Bowl.

₱30 entrance + ₱500 guide fee (split). 1-hour trek. Bring waterproof bag.
Thai street food. Pad Thai, Pork Satay. Everything under ₱200.

Location: The Mansion Estates, Upper Talogtog, San Juan
Notes: Panoramic views of rolling hills & West Philippine Sea. Coffee, dine, and chill. 10-15 min drive from Urbiztondo.
Pizza with longganisa specialty. Casual Italian-Filipino fusion.
Drinks and chill vibes. Kabsat La Union has buy 1 get 1 cocktails.
Chill breakfast at a carinderia or nearby cafe. No rush - enjoy the last La Union morning.

Activity: Last surf or beach walk at Urbiztondo
Notes: Final La Union beach time! Quick surf session, morning swim, or beach walk. Soak it in before heading to the mountains.
Pack up, load car. Checkout time.
Light lunch at a local eatery before the drive. Carinderia or quick meal.
~60km scenic mountain drive via Naguilian Road or Marcos Highway. Beautiful views!
2PM check-in. Drop luggage, freshen up, change into warmer clothes.

Activity: Late lunch at Good Taste Restaurant
Notes: Famous for huge servings at low prices. Filipino-Chinese food. Budget-friendly. First meal in Baguio!

Activity: Visit Mines View Park & Good Shepherd Convent
Notes: ₱10 entrance to Mines View. Buy strawberry jam (₱450-599), ube jam at Good Shepherd.

Activity: Shopping at Good Shepherd pasalubong shops
Notes: Buy ube jam, strawberry products, peanut brittle. Budget ₱600 per person.
Pork Kebab ₱120, big servings. Retro American diner vibe.
Opens 9PM. Very cheap clothes, jackets, vintage items. Budget ₱300-500.
Combo A (Chicken, Spaghetti, Fries, Pizza) ₱140. Heavy meal to fuel the day.
FREE entrance. Strawberry picking ₱450/kg (split). Taxi ₱200 or jeepney ₱20.

Notes: ₱60-90 per meal. Authentic Filipino dishes: adobo, sinigang, tapsilog.

FREE entrance. Chinese temple with pagodas. Open 6AM-5PM. No photos inside temple.

Location: La Azotea Building, Session Road, Baguio
Notes: Authentic Italian. Cappuccino + Tiramisu (₱250). 5-min walk from Hill Station. Cozy trattoria vibe, open till 10PM.
Early breakfast to prep for Mt Kamisong. Famous for dinuguan and sisig.

Activity: Drive to Mt Kamisong (Mt Camisong Forest Park)
Location: Baguio to Sitio Peday, Loacan, Itogon
Notes: 45-min scenic mountain drive from Baguio. Winding roads via Itogon.

Location: Mt Camisong Forest Park, Itogon, Benguet
Notes: 10/10 spot! Glass walkway, Kahilom Hiking Trail, Alapaap Viewing Deck. Brunch at Marahuyo Café (rice bowls) or Alapaap Dining (Strawberry Cansi). ₱500 entrance/person.

Location: Itogon to Session Road area, Baguio
Notes: Drive back 45 min. Quick lunch at carinderia or Session Road area.
Daytime ukay-ukay. Closes 6-7PM. Hunt for vintage clothes and branded items.

Notes: ₱200 entrance. Art museum. 15-30 min from Baguio. Closes 6PM, last entry 5:30PM.
Italian-Filipino cuisine. Classic Spanish taste. ₱₱-₱₱₱.

Activity: Explore Session Road cafes or rest
Notes: Coffee at local cafes or early rest for tomorrow.

Location: 19th Pine St corner La Azotea St, Baguio
Notes: Baguio's best third-wave cafe! Local Benguet beans pour-over, All-Day Breakfast tapsilog, Ube Cheesecake. Minimalist Scandi vibes, free WiFi. Perfect morning chill.
Restored Ifugao & Kalinga native houses. Art galleries, cultural exhibits, weaving demos. ₱100-150 entrance. Great for art & culture lovers!

Location: 5th Floor La Azotea Building, Session Road
Notes: Famous instagrammable restaurant! Whimsical plant-filled interiors. Creative Filipino fusion. A must-visit Baguio dining experience.

Activity: Camp John Hay Eco Trail & Historical Core
Notes: FREE eco trail through pine forests! Bell House, Butterfly Sanctuary, Secret Garden. Optional treetop adventure ₱300+. Peaceful pine forest atmosphere.
Hidden gem! Stone castles, towers depicting Igorot legends, rice terrace walls, viewing decks. ₱100 entrance. Celebrates Cordillera culture.

Activity: More thrift shopping or revisit favorite spots
Notes: Buffer time for last ukay finds or revisiting a favorite spot.

Activity: Dinner at Lemon and Olives Greek Taverna
Notes: Baguio's authentic Greek restaurant! Lamb chops, moussaka, baklava. Garden ambiance. ₱300-700/person. Special dinner experience.
Early rest for final morning adventures tomorrow.

Activity: Breakfast at Cafe by the Ruins
Notes: Iconic Baguio breakfast! Built amid WWII ruins. Famous longganisa, tapa, and brewed coffee. Rustic charming ambiance. A must-try before leaving.

Location: Mount Mary Hill, Romulo Drive, Baguio
Notes: FREE. Stunning pink-domed architecture. Panoramic city views from hilltop. Quick photo stop. Open 5AM-7PM.
FREE. 252 steps to Our Lady of Lourdes statue. Pine tree surroundings. Great morning exercise with rewarding views!
Lush garden with Cordillera indigenous sculptures, pine trees, flower displays. ₱20-50 entrance. Peaceful final Baguio moment.

Last meal in Baguio! Big servings, budget-friendly. One more taste of the city.
Final purchases. Double-check nothing's forgotten.

Activity: Depart Baguio - Drive back to SM Bicutan area
Notes: Afternoon departure via TPLEX-SCTEX. Dinner stop along the expressway. Arrive ~9PM. Safer than late-night driving!
